types.name: String
types.description:
The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8
character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to
represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Int
types.description:
The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric
values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

args.name: asPreview
args.description: Whether to return the Preview Node instead of the Published Node. When the ID of a Node is provided along with asPreview being set to true, the preview node with un-published changes will be returned instead of the published node. If no preview node exists or the requester doesn't have proper capabilities to preview, no node will be returned. If the ID provided is a URI and has a preview query arg, it will be used as a fallback if the "asPreview" argument is not explicitly provided as an argument.

types.name: RootQueryToCategoryConnectionWhereArgs
types.description: Arguments for filtering the RootQueryToCategoryConnection connection

inputFields.name: cacheDomain
inputFields.description: Unique cache key to be produced when this query is stored in an object cache. Default is 'core'.

inputFields.name: childOf
inputFields.description: Term ID to retrieve child terms of. If multiple taxonomies are passed, $child_of is ignored. Default 0.

inputFields.name: childless
inputFields.description: True to limit results to terms that have no children. This parameter has no effect on non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default false.

inputFields.name: descriptionLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the description is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: exclude
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude. If $include is non-empty, $exclude is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: excludeTree
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude along with all of their descendant terms. If $include is non-empty, $exclude_tree is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: hideEmpty
inputFields.description: Whether to hide terms not assigned to any posts. Accepts true or false. Default false

inputFields.name: hierarchical
inputFields.description: Whether to include terms that have non-empty descendants (even if $hide_empty is set to true). Default true.

inputFields.name: include
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to include. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Array of names to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: nameLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the name is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: objectIds
inputFields.description: Array of object IDs. Results will be limited to terms associated with these objects.

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description: Direction the connection should be ordered in

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: Field(s) to order terms by. Defaults to 'name'.

inputFields.name: padCounts
inputFields.description: Whether to pad the quantity of a term's children in the quantity of each term's "count" object variable. Default false.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Parent term ID to retrieve direct-child terms of. Default empty.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Search criteria to match terms. Will be SQL-formatted with wildcards before and after. Default empty.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: Array of slugs to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: termTaxonomyId
inputFields.description: Array of term taxonomy IDs, to match when querying terms.

inputFields.name: updateTermMetaCache
inputFields.description: Whether to prime meta caches for matched terms. Default true.

types.name: ID
types.description:
The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to
refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON
response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable.
When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer
(such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: OrderEnum
types.description: Sort direction for ordered results. Determines whether items are returned in ascending or descending order.

enumValues.name: ASC
enumValues.description: Results ordered from lowest to highest values (i.e. A-Z, oldest-newest)

enumValues.name: DESC
enumValues.description: Results ordered from highest to lowest values (i.e. Z-A, newest-oldest)

types.name: TermObjectsConnectionOrderbyEnum
types.description: Sorting attributes for taxonomy term collections. Determines which property of taxonomy terms is used for ordering results.

enumValues.name: COUNT
enumValues.description: Ordering by number of associated content items.

enumValues.name: DESCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Alphabetical ordering by term description text.

enumValues.name: TERM_GROUP
enumValues.description: Ordering by assigned term grouping value.

enumValues.name: TERM_ORDER
enumValues.description: Ordering by manually defined sort position.

types.name: Connection
types.description: A paginated relationship between objects. Supports cursor-based pagination with edges containing relationship metadata and nodes containing the related objects.

fields.name: edges
fields.description: A list of edges (relational context) between connected nodes

fields.name: nodes
fields.description: A list of connected nodes

fields.name: pageInfo
fields.description: Information about pagination in a connection.

types.name: Edge
types.description: Represents a connection between two objects. Contains both the related object (node) and metadata about the relationship (cursor).

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Opaque reference to the nodes position in the connection. Value can be used with pagination args.

fields.name: node
fields.description: The connected node

types.name: Node
types.description: An object with a globally unique identifier. All objects that can be identified by a unique ID implement this interface.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The globally unique ID for the object

types.name: PageInfo
types.description: Metadata for cursor-based pagination. Provides cursors for continuing pagination and boolean flags indicating if more items exist in either direction.

fields.name: endCursor
fields.description: When paginating forwards, the cursor to continue.

fields.name: hasNextPage
fields.description: When paginating forwards, are there more items?

fields.name: hasPreviousPage
fields.description: When paginating backwards, are there more items?

fields.name: startCursor
fields.description: When paginating backwards, the cursor to continue.

types.name: CategoryConnectionEdge
types.description: Represents a connection to a category. Contains both the category Node and metadata about the relationship.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: Opaque reference to the nodes position in the connection. Value can be used with pagination args.

fields.name: node
fields.description: The connected category Node

types.name: Category
types.description: A taxonomy term that classifies content. Categories support hierarchy and can be used to create a nested structure.

fields.name: ancestors
fields.description: The ancestors of the node. Default ordered as lowest (closest to the child) to highest (closest to the root).

types.name: ScriptLoadingGroupLocationEnum
types.description: Script insertion positions in the document structure. Determines whether scripts are placed in the document head or before the closing body tag.

enumValues.name: FOOTER
enumValues.description: Delayed loading at end of document, right before the closing `<body>` tag. (allows content to render first)

enumValues.name: HEADER
enumValues.description: Early loading in document `<head>` tag. (executes before page content renders)

types.name: ScriptLoadingStrategyEnum
types.description: Script loading optimization attributes. Controls browser behavior for script loading to improve page performance (async or defer).

enumValues.name: ASYNC
enumValues.description: Load script in parallel with page rendering, executing as soon as downloaded

enumValues.name: DEFER
enumValues.description: Download script in parallel but defer execution until page is fully parsed

types.name: CategoryToCategoryConnectionWhereArgs
types.description: Arguments for filtering the CategoryToCategoryConnection connection

inputFields.name: cacheDomain
inputFields.description: Unique cache key to be produced when this query is stored in an object cache. Default is 'core'.

inputFields.name: childOf
inputFields.description: Term ID to retrieve child terms of. If multiple taxonomies are passed, $child_of is ignored. Default 0.

inputFields.name: childless
inputFields.description: True to limit results to terms that have no children. This parameter has no effect on non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default false.

inputFields.name: descriptionLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the description is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: exclude
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude. If $include is non-empty, $exclude is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: excludeTree
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude along with all of their descendant terms. If $include is non-empty, $exclude_tree is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: hideEmpty
inputFields.description: Whether to hide terms not assigned to any posts. Accepts true or false. Default false

inputFields.name: hierarchical
inputFields.description: Whether to include terms that have non-empty descendants (even if $hide_empty is set to true). Default true.

inputFields.name: include
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to include. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Array of names to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: nameLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the name is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: objectIds
inputFields.description: Array of object IDs. Results will be limited to terms associated with these objects.

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description: Direction the connection should be ordered in

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: Field(s) to order terms by. Defaults to 'name'.

inputFields.name: padCounts
inputFields.description: Whether to pad the quantity of a term's children in the quantity of each term's "count" object variable. Default false.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Parent term ID to retrieve direct-child terms of. Default empty.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Search criteria to match terms. Will be SQL-formatted with wildcards before and after. Default empty.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: Array of slugs to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: termTaxonomyId
inputFields.description: Array of term taxonomy IDs, to match when querying terms.

inputFields.name: updateTermMetaCache
inputFields.description: Whether to prime meta caches for matched terms. Default true.

inputFields.description: True for objects with passwords; False for objects without passwords; null for all objects with or without passwords

inputFields.description: Specific database ID of the object

inputFields.description: Array of IDs for the objects to retrieve

inputFields.description: Get objects with a specific mimeType property

inputFields.description: Specify objects to retrieve. Use slugs

inputFields.name: notIn
inputFields.description: Specify IDs NOT to retrieve. If this is used in the same query as "in", it will be ignored

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: What parameter to use to order the objects by.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Use ID to return only children. Use 0 to return only top-level items

inputFields.name: parentIn
inputFields.description: Specify objects whose parent is in an array

inputFields.name: parentNotIn
inputFields.description: Specify posts whose parent is not in an array

inputFields.description: Show posts with a specific status.

types.description: Date values

inputFields.name: day
inputFields.description: Day of the month (from 1 to 31)

inputFields.name: month
inputFields.description: Month number (from 1 to 12)

inputFields.name: year
inputFields.description: 4 digit year (e.g. 2017)

types.name: PostObjectsConnectionDateColumnEnum
types.description: Date field selectors for content filtering. Specifies which date attribute (creation date, modification date) should be used for date-based queries.

enumValues.name: DATE
enumValues.description: The date the comment was created in local time.

enumValues.name: MODIFIED
enumValues.description: The most recent modification date of the comment.

types.name: RelationEnum
types.description: Logical operators for filter conditions. Determines whether multiple filtering criteria should be combined with AND (all must match) or OR (any can match).

enumValues.name: AND
enumValues.description: All conditions must match (more restrictive filtering)

enumValues.name: OR
enumValues.description: Any condition can match (more inclusive filtering)

types.name: MimeTypeEnum
types.description: Media file type classification based on MIME standards. Used to identify and filter media items by their format and content type.

types.name: PostObjectsConnectionOrderbyInput
types.description: Options for ordering the connection

inputFields.name: field
inputFields.description: The field to order the connection by

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description: Possible directions in which to order a list of items

types.name: PostObjectsConnectionOrderbyEnum
types.description: Content sorting attributes for post-type objects. Identifies which content property should be used to determine result order.

enumValues.name: AUTHOR
enumValues.description: Ordering by content author (typically by author name).

enumValues.name: COMMENT_COUNT
enumValues.description: Ordering by popularity based on number of comments.

enumValues.name: IN
enumValues.description: Maintain custom order of IDs exactly as specified in the query with the IN field.

enumValues.name: MENU_ORDER
enumValues.description: Ordering by manually defined sort position.

enumValues.name: NAME_IN
enumValues.description: Maintain custom order of IDs exactly as specified in the query with the NAME_IN field.

enumValues.name: PARENT
enumValues.description: Ordering by parent-child relationship in hierarchical content.

types.name: PostStatusEnum
types.description: Publishing status that controls the visibility and editorial state of content. Determines whether content is published, pending review, in draft state, or private.

enumValues.name: AUTO_DRAFT
enumValues.description: Automatically saved content that has not been manually saved

enumValues.name: DRAFT
enumValues.description: Content that is saved but not yet published or visible to the public

enumValues.name: INHERIT
enumValues.description: Content that inherits its status from a parent object

enumValues.name: PRIVATE
enumValues.description: Content only visible to authorized users with appropriate permissions

enumValues.name: PUBLISH
enumValues.description: Content that is publicly visible to all visitors

enumValues.name: TRASH
enumValues.description: Content marked for deletion but still recoverable

fields.description: The position of this post type in the menu. Only applies if show_in_menu is true.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The internal name of the post type. This should not be used for display purposes.

fields.name: public
fields.description: Whether a content type is intended for use publicly either via the admin interface or by front-end users. While the default settings of exclude_from_search, publicly_queryable, show_ui, and show_in_nav_menus are inherited from public, each does not rely on this relationship and controls a very specific intention.

fields.name: publiclyQueryable
fields.description: Whether queries can be performed on the front end for the content type as part of parse_request().

fields.name: restBase
fields.description: Name of content type to display in REST API "wp/v2" namespace.

fields.name: restControllerClass
fields.description: The REST Controller class assigned to handling this content type.

fields.name: showInAdminBar
fields.description: Makes this content type available via the admin bar.

fields.name: showInGraphql
fields.description: Whether to add the content type to the GraphQL Schema.

fields.name: showInMenu
fields.description: Where to show the content type in the admin menu. To work, $show_ui must be true. If true, the post type is shown in its own top level menu. If false, no menu is shown. If a string of an existing top level menu (eg. "tools.php" or "edit.php?post_type=page"), the post type will be placed as a sub-menu of that.

fields.name: showInNavMenus
fields.description: Makes this content type available for selection in navigation menus.

fields.name: showInRest
fields.description: Whether the content type is associated with a route under the the REST API "wp/v2" namespace.

fields.name: showUi
fields.description: Whether to generate and allow a UI for managing this content type in the admin.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The unique resource identifier path

inputFields.description: Use ID to return only children. Use 0 to return only top-level items

types.name: User
types.description: A registered user account. Users can be assigned roles, author content, and have various capabilities within the site.

fields.name: adminColor
fields.description: The admin color scheme preference for the user. Possible values include "fresh", "light", "blue", "coffee", "ectoplasm", "midnight", "ocean", "sunrise". Default is "fresh".

fields.name: avatar
fields.description: Avatar object for user. The avatar object can be retrieved in different sizes by specifying the size argument.

args.name: size
args.description: The size attribute of the avatar field can be used to fetch avatars of different sizes. The value corresponds to the dimension in pixels to fetch. The default is 96 pixels.

args.name: forceDefault
args.description: Whether to always show the default image, never the Gravatar. Default false

args.name: rating
args.description: The rating level of the avatar.

fields.name: capKey
fields.description: User metadata option name. Usually it will be "wp_capabilities".

fields.name: capabilities
fields.description: A list of capabilities (permissions) granted to the user

fields.name: comments
fields.description: Connection between the User type and the Comment type

fields.name: hasCommentShortcutsEnabled

fields.name: hasRichEditingEnabled

fields.name: hasSyntaxHighlightingEnabled
fields.description: Whether the user has enabled syntax highlighting when editing code within the post editor. Defaults to true.

fields.name: locale

types.name: Commenter
types.description: A user or guest who has submitted a comment. Provides identification and contact information for the comment author.

fields.name: avatar
fields.description: Avatar object for user. The avatar object can be retrieved in different sizes by specifying the size argument.

fields.name: databaseId
fields.description: Identifies the primary key from the database.

fields.name: email
fields.description: The email address of the author of a comment.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The globally unique identifier for the comment author.

fields.name: isRestricted
fields.description: Whether the author information is considered restricted. (not fully public)

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the author of a comment.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The url of the author of a comment.

types.name: Avatar
types.description: Avatars are profile images for users. WordPress by default uses the Gravatar service to host and fetch avatars from.

fields.name: default
fields.description: TEST: URL for the default image or a default type. Accepts '404' (return a 404 instead of a default image), 'retro' (8bit), 'monsterid' (monster), 'wavatar' (cartoon face), 'indenticon' (the 'quilt'), 'mystery', 'mm', or 'mysteryman' (The Oyster Man), 'blank' (transparent GIF), or 'gravatar_default' (the Gravatar logo).

fields.name: extraAttr
fields.description: HTML attributes to insert in the IMG element. Is not sanitized.

fields.name: forceDefault
fields.description: Whether to always show the default image, never the Gravatar.

fields.name: foundAvatar
fields.description: Whether the avatar was successfully found.

fields.name: height
fields.description: Height of the avatar image.

fields.name: isRestricted
fields.description: Whether the object is restricted from the current viewer

fields.name: rating
fields.description: What rating to display avatars up to. Accepts 'G', 'PG', 'R', 'X', and are judged in that order.

fields.name: scheme
fields.description: Type of url scheme to use. Typically HTTP vs. HTTPS.

fields.name: size
fields.description: The size of the avatar in pixels. A value of 96 will match a 96px x 96px gravatar image.

fields.name: url
fields.description: URL for the gravatar image source.

fields.name: width
fields.description: Width of the avatar image.

types.name: AvatarRatingEnum
types.description: Content rating filter for user avatars. Determines the maximum maturity level of avatars to display, following standard content rating classifications (G, PG, R, X).

enumValues.name: G
enumValues.description: Indicates a G level avatar rating level.

enumValues.name: PG
enumValues.description: Indicates a PG level avatar rating level.

enumValues.name: R
enumValues.description: Indicates an R level avatar rating level.

enumValues.name: X
enumValues.description: Indicates an X level avatar rating level.

types.name: CommentsConnectionOrderbyEnum
types.description: Sorting attributes for comment collections. Specifies which comment property determines the order of results.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_AGENT
enumValues.description: Order by browser user agent of the commenter.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_APPROVED
enumValues.description: Order by approval status of the comment.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_AUTHOR
enumValues.description: Order by name of the comment author.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_AUTHOR_EMAIL
enumValues.description: Order by e-mail of the comment author.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_AUTHOR_IP
enumValues.description: Order by IP address of the comment author.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_AUTHOR_URL
enumValues.description: Order by URL address of the comment author.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_DATE
enumValues.description: Chronological ordering by comment submission date.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_DATE_GMT
enumValues.description: Chronological ordering by comment date in UTC/GMT time.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_ID
enumValues.description: Ordering by internal ID (typically reflects creation order).

enumValues.name: COMMENT_IN
enumValues.description: Preserve custom order of IDs as specified in the query.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_KARMA
enumValues.description: Order by the comment karma score.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_PARENT
enumValues.description: Ordering by parent comment relationship (threaded discussions).

enumValues.name: COMMENT_POST_ID
enumValues.description: Ordering by associated content item ID.

enumValues.name: COMMENT_TYPE
enumValues.description: Ordering by comment classification (standard comments, pingbacks, etc.).

enumValues.name: USER_ID
enumValues.description: Ordering by the user account ID associated with the comment as the comment author.

types.name: Comment
types.description: A response or reaction to content submitted by users. Comments are typically associated with a specific content entry.

fields.name: agent
fields.description: User agent used to post the comment. This field is equivalent to WP_Comment->comment_agent and the value matching the "comment_agent" column in SQL.

fields.description: IP address for the author at the time of commenting. This field is equivalent to WP_Comment->comment_author_IP and the value matching the "comment_author_IP" column in SQL.

fields.name: dateGmt
fields.description: Date the comment was posted in GMT. This field is equivalent to WP_Comment->date_gmt and the value matching the "date_gmt" column in SQL.

fields.name: link
fields.description: The permalink of the comment

fields.description: Connection between the Comment type and the Comment type

types.name: PostObjectFieldFormatEnum
types.description: Content field rendering options. Determines whether content fields are returned as raw data or with applied formatting and transformations. Default is RENDERED.

enumValues.description: Content with all formatting and transformations applied, ready for display.

types.name: UserToMediaItemConnectionWhereArgs
types.description: Arguments for filtering the UserToMediaItemConnection connection

inputFields.name: author
inputFields.description: The user that's connected as the author of the object. Use the userId for the author object.

inputFields.name: authorIn
inputFields.description: Find objects connected to author(s) in the array of author's userIds

inputFields.name: authorName
inputFields.description: Find objects connected to the author by the author's nicename

inputFields.name: authorNotIn
inputFields.description: Find objects NOT connected to author(s) in the array of author's userIds

inputFields.name: dateQuery
inputFields.description: Filter the connection based on dates

inputFields.name: hasPassword
inputFields.description: True for objects with passwords; False for objects without passwords; null for all objects with or without passwords

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: Specific database ID of the object

inputFields.name: in
inputFields.description: Array of IDs for the objects to retrieve

inputFields.name: mimeType
inputFields.description: Get objects with a specific mimeType property

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Slug / post_name of the object

inputFields.name: nameIn
inputFields.description: Specify objects to retrieve. Use slugs

inputFields.name: notIn
inputFields.description: Specify IDs NOT to retrieve. If this is used in the same query as "in", it will be ignored

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: What parameter to use to order the objects by.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Use ID to return only children. Use 0 to return only top-level items

inputFields.name: parentIn
inputFields.description: Specify objects whose parent is in an array

inputFields.name: parentNotIn
inputFields.description: Specify posts whose parent is not in an array

inputFields.name: password
inputFields.description: Show posts with a specific password.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Show Posts based on a keyword search

inputFields.name: stati
inputFields.description: Retrieve posts where post status is in an array.

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: Show posts with a specific status.

inputFields.name: title
inputFields.description: Title of the object

inputFields.name: author

inputFields.description: Find objects connected to author(s) in the array of author's userIds

inputFields.description: Find objects connected to the author by the author's nicename

inputFields.name: authorNotIn
inputFields.description: Find objects NOT connected to author(s) in the array of author's userIds

inputFields.description: Filter the connection based on dates

inputFields.name: hasPassword
inputFields.description: True for objects with passwords; False for objects without passwords; null for all objects with or without passwords

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: Specific database ID of the object

inputFields.name: in
inputFields.description: Array of IDs for the objects to retrieve

inputFields.name: mimeType
inputFields.description: Get objects with a specific mimeType property

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Slug / post_name of the object

inputFields.name: nameIn
inputFields.description: Specify objects to retrieve. Use slugs

inputFields.name: notIn
inputFields.description: Specify IDs NOT to retrieve. If this is used in the same query as "in", it will be ignored

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: What parameter to use to order the objects by.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Use ID to return only children. Use 0 to return only top-level items

inputFields.name: parentIn
inputFields.description: Specify objects whose parent is in an array

inputFields.name: parentNotIn
inputFields.description: Specify posts whose parent is not in an array

inputFields.description: Show posts with a specific password.

inputFields.description: Show Posts based on a keyword search

inputFields.description: Retrieve posts where post status is in an array.

inputFields.description: Show posts with a specific status.

types.name: PostToCategoryConnectionWhereArgs
types.description: Arguments for filtering the PostToCategoryConnection connection

inputFields.name: cacheDomain
inputFields.description: Unique cache key to be produced when this query is stored in an object cache. Default is 'core'.

inputFields.name: childOf
inputFields.description: Term ID to retrieve child terms of. If multiple taxonomies are passed, $child_of is ignored. Default 0.

inputFields.name: childless
inputFields.description: True to limit results to terms that have no children. This parameter has no effect on non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default false.

inputFields.name: descriptionLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the description is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: exclude
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude. If $include is non-empty, $exclude is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: excludeTree
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude along with all of their descendant terms. If $include is non-empty, $exclude_tree is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: hideEmpty
inputFields.description: Whether to hide terms not assigned to any posts. Accepts true or false. Default false

inputFields.name: hierarchical
inputFields.description: Whether to include terms that have non-empty descendants (even if $hide_empty is set to true). Default true.

inputFields.name: include
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to include. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Array of names to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: nameLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the name is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: objectIds
inputFields.description: Array of object IDs. Results will be limited to terms associated with these objects.

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description: Direction the connection should be ordered in

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: Field(s) to order terms by. Defaults to 'name'.

inputFields.name: padCounts
inputFields.description: Whether to pad the quantity of a term's children in the quantity of each term's "count" object variable. Default false.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Parent term ID to retrieve direct-child terms of. Default empty.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Search criteria to match terms. Will be SQL-formatted with wildcards before and after. Default empty.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: Array of slugs to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: termTaxonomyId
inputFields.description: Array of term taxonomy IDs, to match when querying terms.

inputFields.name: updateTermMetaCache
inputFields.description: Whether to prime meta caches for matched terms. Default true.

types.name: PostToPostFormatConnectionWhereArgs
types.description: Arguments for filtering the PostToPostFormatConnection connection

inputFields.name: cacheDomain
inputFields.description: Unique cache key to be produced when this query is stored in an object cache. Default is 'core'.

inputFields.name: childOf
inputFields.description: Term ID to retrieve child terms of. If multiple taxonomies are passed, $child_of is ignored. Default 0.

inputFields.name: childless
inputFields.description: True to limit results to terms that have no children. This parameter has no effect on non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default false.

inputFields.name: descriptionLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the description is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: exclude
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude. If $include is non-empty, $exclude is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: excludeTree
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude along with all of their descendant terms. If $include is non-empty, $exclude_tree is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: hideEmpty
inputFields.description: Whether to hide terms not assigned to any posts. Accepts true or false. Default false

inputFields.name: hierarchical
inputFields.description: Whether to include terms that have non-empty descendants (even if $hide_empty is set to true). Default true.

inputFields.name: include
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to include. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Array of names to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: nameLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the name is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: objectIds
inputFields.description: Array of object IDs. Results will be limited to terms associated with these objects.

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description: Direction the connection should be ordered in

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: Field(s) to order terms by. Defaults to 'name'.

inputFields.name: padCounts
inputFields.description: Whether to pad the quantity of a term's children in the quantity of each term's "count" object variable. Default false.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Parent term ID to retrieve direct-child terms of. Default empty.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Search criteria to match terms. Will be SQL-formatted with wildcards before and after. Default empty.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: Array of slugs to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: termTaxonomyId
inputFields.description: Array of term taxonomy IDs, to match when querying terms.

inputFields.name: updateTermMetaCache
inputFields.description: Whether to prime meta caches for matched terms. Default true.

types.name: PostToTermNodeConnectionWhereArgs
types.description: Arguments for filtering the PostToTermNodeConnection connection

inputFields.name: cacheDomain
inputFields.description: Unique cache key to be produced when this query is stored in an object cache. Default is 'core'.

inputFields.name: childOf
inputFields.description: Term ID to retrieve child terms of. If multiple taxonomies are passed, $child_of is ignored. Default 0.

inputFields.name: childless
inputFields.description: True to limit results to terms that have no children. This parameter has no effect on non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default false.

inputFields.name: descriptionLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the description is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: exclude
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude. If $include is non-empty, $exclude is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: excludeTree
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude along with all of their descendant terms. If $include is non-empty, $exclude_tree is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: hideEmpty
inputFields.description: Whether to hide terms not assigned to any posts. Accepts true or false. Default false

inputFields.name: hierarchical
inputFields.description: Whether to include terms that have non-empty descendants (even if $hide_empty is set to true). Default true.

inputFields.name: include
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to include. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Array of names to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: nameLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the name is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: objectIds
inputFields.description: Array of object IDs. Results will be limited to terms associated with these objects.

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description: Direction the connection should be ordered in

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: Field(s) to order terms by. Defaults to 'name'.

inputFields.name: padCounts
inputFields.description: Whether to pad the quantity of a term's children in the quantity of each term's "count" object variable. Default false.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Parent term ID to retrieve direct-child terms of. Default empty.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Search criteria to match terms. Will be SQL-formatted with wildcards before and after. Default empty.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: Array of slugs to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: taxonomies
inputFields.description: The Taxonomy to filter terms by

inputFields.name: termTaxonomyId
inputFields.description: Array of term taxonomy IDs, to match when querying terms.

inputFields.name: updateTermMetaCache
inputFields.description: Whether to prime meta caches for matched terms. Default true.

types.description: Available classification systems for organizing content. Identifies the different taxonomy types that can be used for content categorization.

enumValues.name: MUST_USE

enumValues.name: PAUSED

enumValues.name: UPGRADE

types.description: Identifier types for retrieving a specific Post. Specifies which unique attribute is used to find an exact Post.

enumValues.description: Identify a resource by the Database ID.

enumValues.name: ID
enumValues.description: Identify a resource by the (hashed) Global ID.

enumValues.name: SLUG
enumValues.description: Identify a resource by the slug. Available to non-hierarchcial Types where the slug is a unique identifier.

enumValues.name: URI
enumValues.description: Identify a resource by the URI.

types.name: PostFormatIdType
types.description: Identifier types for retrieving a specific PostFormat. Determines which unique property (global ID, database ID, slug, etc.) is used to locate the PostFormat.

enumValues.name: DATABASE_ID
enumValues.description: The Database ID for the node

enumValues.name: SLUG
enumValues.description: Url friendly name of the node

types.name: RootQueryToPostFormatConnectionWhereArgs
types.description: Arguments for filtering the RootQueryToPostFormatConnection connection

inputFields.name: cacheDomain
inputFields.description: Unique cache key to be produced when this query is stored in an object cache. Default is 'core'.

inputFields.name: childOf
inputFields.description: Term ID to retrieve child terms of. If multiple taxonomies are passed, $child_of is ignored. Default 0.

inputFields.name: childless
inputFields.description: True to limit results to terms that have no children. This parameter has no effect on non-hierarchical taxonomies. Default false.

inputFields.name: descriptionLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the description is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: exclude
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude. If $include is non-empty, $exclude is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: excludeTree
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to exclude along with all of their descendant terms. If $include is non-empty, $exclude_tree is ignored. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: hideEmpty
inputFields.description: Whether to hide terms not assigned to any posts. Accepts true or false. Default false

inputFields.name: hierarchical
inputFields.description: Whether to include terms that have non-empty descendants (even if $hide_empty is set to true). Default true.

inputFields.name: include
inputFields.description: Array of term ids to include. Default empty array.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Array of names to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: nameLike
inputFields.description: Retrieve terms where the name is LIKE the input value. Default empty.

inputFields.name: objectIds
inputFields.description: Array of object IDs. Results will be limited to terms associated with these objects.

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description: Direction the connection should be ordered in

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: Field(s) to order terms by. Defaults to 'name'.

inputFields.name: padCounts
inputFields.description: Whether to pad the quantity of a term's children in the quantity of each term's "count" object variable. Default false.

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Parent term ID to retrieve direct-child terms of. Default empty.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Search criteria to match terms. Will be SQL-formatted with wildcards before and after. Default empty.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: Array of slugs to return term(s) for. Default empty.

inputFields.name: termTaxonomyId
inputFields.description: Array of term taxonomy IDs, to match when querying terms.

inputFields.name: updateTermMetaCache
inputFields.description: Whether to prime meta caches for matched terms. Default true.

types.name: Theme
types.description: A theme object

fields.name: author
fields.description: Name of the theme author(s), could also be a company name. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get( "Author" ).

fields.name: authorUri
fields.description: URI for the author/company website. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get( "AuthorURI" ).

fields.name: description
fields.description: The description of the theme. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get( "Description" ).

fields.name: id
fields.description: The globally unique identifier of the theme object.

fields.name: isRestricted
fields.description: Whether the object is restricted from the current viewer

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name of the theme. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get( "Name" ).

fields.name: screenshot
fields.description: The URL of the screenshot for the theme. The screenshot is intended to give an overview of what the theme looks like. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get_screenshot().

fields.name: slug
fields.description: The theme slug is used to internally match themes. Theme slugs can have subdirectories like: my-theme/sub-theme. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get_stylesheet().

fields.name: tags
fields.description: URI for the author/company website. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get( "Tags" ).

fields.name: themeUri
fields.description: A URI if the theme has a website associated with it. The Theme URI is handy for directing users to a theme site for support etc. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get( "ThemeURI" ).

fields.name: version
fields.description: The current version of the theme. This field is equivalent to WP_Theme->get( "Version" ).

types.name: RootQueryToUserConnectionWhereArgs
types.description: Arguments for filtering the RootQueryToUserConnection connection

inputFields.name: exclude
inputFields.description: Array of userIds to exclude.

inputFields.name: hasPublishedPosts
inputFields.description: Pass an array of post types to filter results to users who have published posts in those post types.

inputFields.name: include
inputFields.description: Array of userIds to include.

inputFields.name: login
inputFields.description: The user login.

inputFields.name: loginIn
inputFields.description: An array of logins to include. Users matching one of these logins will be included in results.

inputFields.name: loginNotIn
inputFields.description: An array of logins to exclude. Users matching one of these logins will not be included in results.

inputFields.name: nicename
inputFields.description: The user nicename.

inputFields.name: nicenameIn
inputFields.description: An array of nicenames to include. Users matching one of these nicenames will be included in results.

inputFields.name: nicenameNotIn
inputFields.description: An array of nicenames to exclude. Users matching one of these nicenames will not be included in results.

inputFields.name: orderby
inputFields.description: What parameter to use to order the objects by.

inputFields.name: role
inputFields.description: An array of role names that users must match to be included in results. Note that this is an inclusive list: users must match *each* role.

inputFields.name: roleIn
inputFields.description: An array of role names. Matched users must have at least one of these roles.

inputFields.name: roleNotIn
inputFields.description: An array of role names to exclude. Users matching one or more of these roles will not be included in results.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Search keyword. Searches for possible string matches on columns. When "searchColumns" is left empty, it tries to determine which column to search in based on search string.

inputFields.name: searchColumns
inputFields.description: Array of column names to be searched. Accepts 'ID', 'login', 'nicename', 'email', 'url'.

types.name: UsersConnectionOrderbyInput
types.description: Options for ordering the connection

inputFields.name: field
inputFields.description: The field name used to sort the results.

inputFields.name: order
inputFields.description: The cardinality of the order of the connection

types.name: UsersConnectionOrderbyEnum
types.description: User attribute sorting options. Determines which property of user accounts is used for ordering user listings.

enumValues.name: LOGIN_IN
enumValues.description: Preserve the login order given in the LOGIN_IN array

enumValues.name: NICE_NAME_IN
enumValues.description: Preserve the nice name order given in the NICE_NAME_IN array

types.name: UserRoleEnum
types.description: Permission levels for user accounts. Defines the standard access levels that control what actions users can perform within the system.

types.name: UsersConnectionSearchColumnEnum
types.description: User properties that can be targeted in search operations. Defines which user attributes can be searched when looking for specific users.

enumValues.name: LOGIN
enumValues.description: The username the User uses to login with.

enumValues.name: NICENAME
enumValues.description: A URL-friendly name for the user. The default is the user's username.

enumValues.name: URL
enumValues.description: The URL of the user's website.

types.name: CreateCategoryInput
types.description: Input for the createCategory mutation.

inputFields.name: aliasOf
inputFields.description: The slug that the category will be an alias of

inputFields.name: clientMutationId
inputFields.description: This is an ID that can be passed to a mutation by the client to track the progress of mutations and catch possible duplicate mutation submissions.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the category object

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the category object to mutate

inputFields.name: parentDatabaseId
inputFields.description: The database ID of the category that should be set as the parent. This field cannot be used in conjunction with parentId

inputFields.name: parentId
inputFields.description: The ID of the category that should be set as the parent. This field cannot be used in conjunction with parentDatabaseId

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: If this argument exists then the slug will be checked to see if it is not an existing valid term. If that check succeeds (it is not a valid term), then it is added and the term id is given. If it fails, then a check is made to whether the taxonomy is hierarchical and the parent argument is not empty. If the second check succeeds, the term will be inserted and the term id will be given. If the slug argument is empty, then it will be calculated from the term name.

types.name: CreateCategoryPayload
types.description: The payload for the createCategory mutation.

fields.name: category
fields.description: The created category

fields.name: clientMutationId
fields.description: If a 'clientMutationId' input is provided to the mutation, it will be returned as output on the mutation. This ID can be used by the client to track the progress of mutations and catch possible duplicate mutation submissions.

types.name: CreateCommentInput
types.description: Input for the createComment mutation.

inputFields.name: author
inputFields.description: The name of the comment's author.

inputFields.name: authorEmail
inputFields.description: The email of the comment's author.

inputFields.name: authorUrl
inputFields.description: The url of the comment's author.

inputFields.name: clientMutationId
inputFields.description: This is an ID that can be passed to a mutation by the client to track the progress of mutations and catch possible duplicate mutation submissions.

inputFields.name: commentOn
inputFields.description: The database ID of the post object the comment belongs to.

inputFields.name: content
inputFields.description: Content of the comment.

inputFields.name: date
inputFields.description: The date of the object. Preferable to enter as year/month/day ( e.g. 01/31/2017 ) as it will rearrange date as fit if it is not specified. Incomplete dates may have unintended results for example, "2017" as the input will use current date with timestamp 20:17

inputFields.name: parent
inputFields.description: Parent comment ID of current comment.

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: The approval status of the comment

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description: Type of comment.

types.name: CreateCommentPayload
types.description: The payload for the createComment mutation.

fields.name: clientMutationId
fields.description: If a 'clientMutationId' input is provided to the mutation, it will be returned as output on the mutation. This ID can be used by the client to track the progress of mutations and catch possible duplicate mutation submissions.

fields.name: comment
fields.description: The comment that was created

fields.name: success
fields.description: Whether the mutation succeeded. If the comment is not approved, the server will not return the comment to a non authenticated user, but a success message can be returned if the create succeeded, and the client can optimistically add the comment to the client cache

types.name: PostCategoriesInput
types.description: Set relationships between the post to categories

inputFields.name: append
inputFields.description: If true, this will append the category to existing related categories. If false, this will replace existing relationships. Default true.

inputFields.name: nodes
inputFields.description: The input list of items to set.

types.name: PostCategoriesNodeInput
types.description: List of categories to connect the post to. If an ID is set, it will be used to create the connection. If not, it will look for a slug. If neither are valid existing terms, and the site is configured to allow terms to be created during post mutations, a term will be created using the Name if it exists in the input, then fallback to the slug if it exists.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the category. This field is used to set a description of the category if a new one is created during the mutation.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The ID of the category. If present, this will be used to connect to the post. If no existing category exists with this ID, no connection will be made.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the category. This field is used to create a new term, if term creation is enabled in nested mutations, and if one does not already exist with the provided slug or ID or if a slug or ID is not provided. If no name is included and a term is created, the creation will fallback to the slug field.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: The slug of the category. If no ID is present, this field will be used to make a connection. If no existing term exists with this slug, this field will be used as a fallback to the Name field when creating a new term to connect to, if term creation is enabled as a nested mutation.

types.name: PostPostFormatsInput
types.description: Set relationships between the post to postFormats

inputFields.name: append
inputFields.description: If true, this will append the postFormat to existing related postFormats. If false, this will replace existing relationships. Default true.

inputFields.name: nodes
inputFields.description: The input list of items to set.

types.name: PostPostFormatsNodeInput
types.description: List of postFormats to connect the post to. If an ID is set, it will be used to create the connection. If not, it will look for a slug. If neither are valid existing terms, and the site is configured to allow terms to be created during post mutations, a term will be created using the Name if it exists in the input, then fallback to the slug if it exists.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the postFormat. This field is used to set a description of the postFormat if a new one is created during the mutation.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The ID of the postFormat. If present, this will be used to connect to the post. If no existing postFormat exists with this ID, no connection will be made.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the postFormat. This field is used to create a new term, if term creation is enabled in nested mutations, and if one does not already exist with the provided slug or ID or if a slug or ID is not provided. If no name is included and a term is created, the creation will fallback to the slug field.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: The slug of the postFormat. If no ID is present, this field will be used to make a connection. If no existing term exists with this slug, this field will be used as a fallback to the Name field when creating a new term to connect to, if term creation is enabled as a nested mutation.

types.name: PostTagsInput
types.description: Set relationships between the post to tags

inputFields.name: append
inputFields.description: If true, this will append the tag to existing related tags. If false, this will replace existing relationships. Default true.

inputFields.name: nodes
inputFields.description: The input list of items to set.

types.name: PostTagsNodeInput
types.description: List of tags to connect the post to. If an ID is set, it will be used to create the connection. If not, it will look for a slug. If neither are valid existing terms, and the site is configured to allow terms to be created during post mutations, a term will be created using the Name if it exists in the input, then fallback to the slug if it exists.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the tag. This field is used to set a description of the tag if a new one is created during the mutation.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The ID of the tag. If present, this will be used to connect to the post. If no existing tag exists with this ID, no connection will be made.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the tag. This field is used to create a new term, if term creation is enabled in nested mutations, and if one does not already exist with the provided slug or ID or if a slug or ID is not provided. If no name is included and a term is created, the creation will fallback to the slug field.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: The slug of the tag. If no ID is present, this field will be used to make a connection. If no existing term exists with this slug, this field will be used as a fallback to the Name field when creating a new term to connect to, if term creation is enabled as a nested mutation.

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: If this argument exists then the slug will be checked to see if it is not an existing valid term. If that check succeeds (it is not a valid term), then it is added and the term id is given. If it fails, then a check is made to whether the taxonomy is hierarchical and the parent argument is not empty. If the second check succeeds, the term will be inserted and the term id will be given. If the slug argument is empty, then it will be calculated from the term name.

types.name: UpdateTagInput
types.description: Input for the updateTag mutation.

inputFields.name: aliasOf
inputFields.description: The slug that the post_tag will be an alias of

inputFields.name: clientMutationId
inputFields.description: This is an ID that can be passed to a mutation by the client to track the progress of mutations and catch possible duplicate mutation submissions.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the post_tag object

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The ID of the tag object to update

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the post_tag object to mutate

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: If this argument exists then the slug will be checked to see if it is not an existing valid term. If that check succeeds (it is not a valid term), then it is added and the term id is given. If it fails, then a check is made to whether the taxonomy is hierarchical and the parent argument is not empty. If the second check succeeds, the term will be inserted and the term id will be given. If the slug argument is empty, then it will be calculated from the term name.

inputFields.name: aim
inputFields.description: User's AOL IM account.

inputFields.name: clientMutationId
inputFields.description: This is an ID that can be passed to a mutation by the client to track the progress of mutations and catch possible duplicate mutation submissions.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: A string containing content about the user.

inputFields.name: displayName
inputFields.description: A string that will be shown on the site. Defaults to user's username. It is likely that you will want to change this, for both appearance and security through obscurity (that is if you dont use and delete the default admin user).

inputFields.name: email
inputFields.description: A string containing the user's email address.

inputFields.name: firstName
inputFields.description: The user's first name.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The ID of the user

inputFields.name: jabber
inputFields.description: User's Jabber account.

inputFields.name: lastName
inputFields.description: The user's last name.

inputFields.name: locale
inputFields.description: User's locale.

inputFields.name: nicename
inputFields.description: A string that contains a URL-friendly name for the user. The default is the user's username.

inputFields.name: nickname
inputFields.description: The user's nickname, defaults to the user's username.

inputFields.name: password
inputFields.description: A string that contains the plain text password for the user.

inputFields.name: registered
inputFields.description: The date the user registered. Format is Y-m-d H:i:s.

inputFields.name: richEditing
inputFields.description: A string for whether to enable the rich editor or not. False if not empty.

inputFields.name: roles
inputFields.description: An array of roles to be assigned to the user.

inputFields.name: websiteUrl
inputFields.description: A string containing the user's URL for the user's web site.

inputFields.name: yim
inputFields.description: User's Yahoo IM account.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name and description, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.
